title: ipv6_addr.h struct in6_addr detection cascade lacks summary
description:
In ipv6_addr.h:31-79, there is a long preprocessor cascade for detecting struct in6_addr. The rationale comments are excellent (lines 36-54), but the actual #if/#elif chain spans 23 lines with no summary of what each branch does.

What goes wrong: When adding support for a new platform, a developer must trace through each condition to understand where their platform falls. The block would benefit from a summary table.

Suggested fix: Add a brief structured comment before the cascade:
```c
/*
 * Detection order:
 *   1. LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR       - explicit user override
 *   2. IN6ADDR_ANY_INIT           - POSIX-compliant header included
 *   3. CONFIG_NET_IPV6 + Zephyr   - include zephyr/net/net_ip.h
 *   4. Platform header guards     - system in6_addr already defined
 *   5. Fallback                   - provide our own
 */
```
close_reason: Added structured table documenting all 5 detection priority levels with their checks, actions, and use cases
